task: I’m putting together a punch list for a rehab unit, and I want a simple way to make sure nothing gets signed off too early. What checkpoints would you use so I can catch defects, missing paperwork, or scope creep before I pay anyone?

turns.agent_response:
Use a **stage-gate punch list**. Don’t sign off by “feel” — sign off only when each gate clears.

## 1) Pre-closeout gate: paperwork first
Before any final payment, confirm you have:
- signed scope / change orders
- permit sign-offs / inspections, if applicable
- lien waivers from all subs
- warranty info / manuals
- final invoice tied to approved scope
- before/after photos for hidden work

**Stop sign:** missing change orders, unsigned extras, or “we’ll send it later” paperwork.

## 2) Rough-in gate: hidden work
Check these before walls close:
- plumbing pressure test / no active leaks
- electrical rough-in matches plan
- HVAC lines, drains, condensate, vents correct
- framing / blocking where needed
- fire/safety items installed if required

**Stop sign:** anything hidden that can’t be verified now.

## 3) Drywall / enclosure gate
Before paint and finishes:
- drywall hung, taped, sanded properly
- no bulges, cracks, nail pops
- penetrations sealed
- wet areas properly waterproofed
- cabinets / fixtures will fit the rough openings

**Stop sign:** finish work starting before substrate is right.

## 4) Finish gate: visible quality
Walk every room and test everything:
- doors open/close, latch, align
- floors even, no damage, no squeaks
- paint coverage consistent
- trim caulked, corners clean
- outlets, switches, lights work
- plumbing fixtures run hot/cold, no leaks
- appliances installed and functional
- smoke/CO detectors working

**Stop sign:** cosmetic cover-ups over functional defects.

## 5) Scope creep gate: compare to contract
For every item, ask:
- was it in the original scope?
- if not, was there a written change order?
- was price approved before work started?
- does the invoice match the approved change?

**Stop sign:** “small extras” with no paper trail.

## 6) Final acceptance gate: no money until all three match
Only pay when these reconcile:
- **scope** = what was promised
- **field** = what was actually done
- **invoice** = what they’re billing

If any one of those is off, hold payment.

## Simple rule
**No sign-off until:**
1. work is visible and tested  
2. paperwork is complete  
3. any extras are approved in writing  
4. punch items are closed  
5. lien waivers are in hand  

## Fast practical format
Use a punch list with columns:
- Item
- Trade
- Location
- Defect
- Priority
- Who owns it
- Due date
- Re-inspection date
- Closed by

## Hard red flags
Walk back sign-off if you see:
- incomplete paperwork
- “minor” items that affect function
- repeated callbacks on the same trade
- unfinished work hidden by paint or trim
- invoices larger than approved scope
- pressure to pay before inspection

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page rehab closeout checklist** you can use on-site.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good, but I’d tighten it. You gave the right gates, but I want the actual hold points spelled out in a way a GC can’t talk around.

Add this:
- no final pay until the punch list is signed by **you**, not just the superintendent
- any concealed work gets photo proof before it’s covered
- no CO / final acceptance until permit closeout, final inspection sign-offs, and lien waivers are all in hand
- anything on the invoice that isn’t in the signed scope gets deleted or gets a written change order, no discussion
- one re-walk after corrections, then payment; no endless back-and-forth

Also separate **defect**, **paperwork**, and **scope creep** into three buckets. That’s the part that keeps people from muddying it up.

If you want, turn it into a tighter one-page checklist with yes/no boxes and a payment hold line at the bottom.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **one-page rehab closeout checklist** with hard hold points and three clean buckets.

---

# Rehab Closeout Checklist
**No final payment until all items below are checked YES by owner/manager, not just superintendent.**

## 1) DEFECTS
### Visible work
- [ ] Doors, windows, cabinets, trim, and finishes installed correctly
- [ ] Paint, patching, caulk, and flooring are complete and acceptable
- [ ] No damage, gaps, cracks, stains, or unfinished areas
- [ ] Fixtures are secure and properly installed

### Functional work
- [ ] Plumbing runs hot/cold, no leaks, drains properly
- [ ] Electrical outlets, switches, lights, and GFCIs work
- [ ] HVAC operates correctly, vents/returns are installed, no obvious issues
- [ ] Appliances function as intended
- [ ] Smoke/CO detectors installed and working

### Concealed work
- [ ] Any hidden work was photo-documented before it was covered
- [ ] No concealed defects were buried under drywall, paint, trim, or flooring

**Hold point:** any unresolved defect stays open until corrected and re-walked.

---

## 2) PAPERWORK
- [ ] Signed scope is on file
- [ ] All change orders are signed before work was performed
- [ ] Final invoice matches approved scope and approved change orders
- [ ] Permit closeout is complete
- [ ] Final inspection sign-offs are in hand
- [ ] Lien waivers received from GC and all subs
- [ ] Warranty info / manuals delivered, if applicable
- [ ] Any required photo documentation is included

**Hard rule:** no CO, no final acceptance.

**Hold point:** no final pay until permit closeout, final inspection sign-offs, and lien waivers are all received.

---

## 3) SCOPE CREEP
For every line item on the invoice:
- [ ] Item was in the signed scope, or
- [ ] Item has a written change order signed before execution

**If not:**
- [ ] Delete the line item, or
- [ ] Get a written change order

**No discussion rule:** anything billed but not in the signed scope gets removed unless documented in writing.

---

## 4) RE-WALK / CLOSEOUT
- [ ] Punch list was signed by you
- [ ] Corrections were completed
- [ ] One re-walk was done after corrections
- [ ] No open items remain
- [ ] No further back-and-forth required

**Rule:** one correction cycle, one re-walk, then payment decision.

---

## PAYMENT HOLD LINE
**Payment is held until all of the following are true:**
1. Punch list is signed by owner/manager  
2. Concealed work is photo-documented before cover-up  
3. Permit closeout and final inspection sign-offs are complete  
4. Lien waivers are in hand  
5. Invoice matches signed scope and approved change orders  
6. All punch items are closed after one re-walk  

---

## Quick yes/no closeout line
- [ ] Defects closed
- [ ] Paperwork complete
- [ ] Scope matches invoice
- [ ] Re-walk passed
- [ ] Final payment approved

If you want, I can turn this into a **clean printable version** with a simple table layout for site use.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the structure I wanted. Clean enough that a GC can’t wriggle out of it, and the three buckets keep the arguments from getting mixed up.

The only thing I’d maybe add is a line for **who signed what and when** — because people get cute later and act like a verbal okay counts. But as written, it’s usable.
